calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Evidentiary Hearing
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jul-28-2023 Text Ruling

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
Off Calendar Jun-13-2023 Continued To Jul-28-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Weaver appearing by zoom for Petitioners.
Mary Mellinger appearing by zoom (pro per).
Jodi Phillips appearing via zoom for Jon Vaught.

Parties presented oral argument in regard to the amount of rent that was being charged and whether a higher amount should have been paid by Mrs. Mellinger. Mrs. Mellinger stated that the $2,000.00 for rent was an agreement she made with her mother. Mr. Weaver requests for an evidentiary hearing and that it shall take no longer than 3 to 4 hours. First and final report of administrator and request for approval of first and final accounting etc., is continued to July 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Evidentiary hearing is also set for July 28, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Parties to file witness list and exhibit list one week before the hearing on July 21, 2023.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Tan. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Continued To Mar-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-07-2022 Continued To Mar-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Weaver appeared by zoom for Randolph Mellinger.
John Di Ana appeared by zoom for and with Mary Mellinger by zoom.

Mr. Di Ana stated that they have had significant conversation, that Mr. Weaver has worked with him in a very cooperative fashion to try to work out the issues, that there has been a significant amount of litigation back forth in a trust matter and that there is no trust between the parties. Mr. Di Ana also stated that Mr. Weaver's client does not trust that his client will administer the estate properly and treat her fairly, and that she fears that her brother will withhold a distribution from a trust that she needs to purchase the condo. Mr. Weaver stated that this is now Ms. Mellinger's fifth attorney, that she has filed litigation against her five siblings who are in line, that the mother changed Ms. Mellinger's share to include her children to receive from her share, and that she has opposed every step of the way and every action. Mr. Weaver also stated that all of the parties are asking to have Randy appointed, that he is willing to waive his fee as administrator, that there are some significant concerns that she hasn't paid rent for a couple of years, and that five out the six siblings want Randy. Mr. DiAna stated that rent is owed to the trust, that Ms. Mellinger has been putting that money aside, that she is owed money from rent from another property and that she feels it evens out. The court expressed concern about appointing either party and there being further litigation, and questions why no one is asking to have an independent party appointed. The Petitions for Letters of Administration (2) are continued to March 7, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to pick someone. The court noted that either party can file a petition to appoint a neutral administrator.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: G. Bastain. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Continued To Jan-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-13-2021 Continued To Jan-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jack Weaver, via ZOOM, for G. Randolph Mellinger, Terry Wyrsch, Nancy Warner, Steven Mellinger, and Kathy Herbert, Petitioners; Mary Mellinger, via ZOOM, in propria persona, Petitioner.

The court queries the parties regarding whether there have been discussions regarding how they plan on proceeding. Mr. Weaver reports that he has not spoken to opposing counsel in quite some time. Ms. Mellinger reports that she has just recently retained new counsel but that he could not appear this morning due to a conflict. The court then points Mr. Weaver to the examiner's notes, particularly regarding the signatures on his petition which appear to be computer fonts and not original signatures. Mr. Weaver reports that he has not met with his clients in person for some time due to the pandemic and that he will work on addressing the examiner's notes. The court continues to discuss the current position of this matter with Mr. Weaver and Ms. Mellinger and encourages the parties to meet and confer to settle this matter or agree as to who will serve as Administrator, agree to jointly serve as co-Administrators, or agree to a neutral fiduciary to avoid difficulties administering the estate.

After additional discussion, both petitions on today's calendar are continued to Jan-24-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
